title: Pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama
what_description: Pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is characterized by its rosette of leaves, with leaf blades that are linear to lanceolate in shape, 5-20 mm long, and covered with a dense indumentum of white, woolly hairs. The inflorescence is a corymb of small, yellow flowers, 1-2 mm in diameter, with 5-7 yellow ray florets and 20-50 yellow disc florets. The plant grows up to 5-15 cm tall, and its stems and leaves are covered with the same type of indumentum as the leaf blades. Pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama is typically found in dry to mesic areas of subalpine to alpine zones.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama are skin inflammation, digestive issues, wounds and rheumatism, which are alleviated due to its anti-inflammatory and antirheumatic properties. Fever reduction and fungal infections can be treated with this plant due to its antipyretic and antifungal compounds. Additionally, Pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama is effective in treating diarrhea, cough, gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems due to its anti-diarrheal and expectorant properties. It also possesses antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which aid in wound healing and skin conditions such as eczema. Furthermore, this plant is used to treat skin irritation, pain relief, allergic reactions and gastroenteritis, making it a versatile remedy for various health issues.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama are the antioxidant activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, that neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress. This plant also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, mediated by sesquiterpene lactones, that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Its antimicrobial activity, due to the presence of saponins and volatile oils, has been shown to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi. Additionally, Pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama has been found to possess analgesic properties, attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, that reduce pain perception by inhibiting the production of pain-related neurotransmitters. The hepatoprotective activity of this plant is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which protect liver cells from damage and oxidative stress caused by various toxins.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama are flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and phenolic acids like ferulic and caffeic acids, with their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Additionally, taraxasterol, a triterpenoid, exhibits significant anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as tannins, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Furthermore, the presence of triterpenoids, including taraxasterol and 6β-hydroxytaraxasterol, contributes to the plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. These constituents are responsible for the plant's therapeutic effects, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The presence of these healing constituents makes Pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama a valuable resource for the development of new medicinal products.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama are typically made from its aerial parts, which are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. An infusion is prepared by steeping the plant material in hot water to make a tea, often used to treat gastrointestinal issues. A tincture is made by soaking the plant material in a solvent, such as ethanol or glycerol, to create a concentrated liquid extract. A decoction is prepared by boiling the plant material to extract its active compounds, often used to treat inflammatory conditions. Additionally, a salve can be made by infusing the plant material in a carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil, to create a topical treatment for skin issues.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama improperly include inducing nausea and causing diarrhea due to its potential toxicity and high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and triterpenoid saponins, which can irritate the digestive system and disrupt normal gut function. Additionally, pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama can trigger allergic reactions and develop skin irritation in some individuals who are sensitive to its allergenic compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and other secondary metabolites. These compounds can cause an immune response, leading to symptoms like redness, itching, and swelling of the skin. Furthermore, improper use of pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama can lead to nausea, as the toxic compounds can irritate the stomach and cause vomiting. If not handled carefully, pilosella leucopsilon subsp. pilisquama can pose serious health risks, especially for those with pre-existing medical conditions or sensitivities.
